scheme_name: Advanced Animal Breeding Scheme (Supply of Breedable Pedigree Murrah Bull on Subsidy)
page_title: Advanced Animal Breeding Scheme (Supply of Breedable Pedigree Murrah Bull on Subsidy)

details: "Advanced Animal Breeding Scheme (Supply of Breedable Pedigree Murrah Bulls on Subsidy)" by the Animal Husbandry Department, Madhya Pradesh aims to improve cattle breeding by supplying pedigreed breedable Murrah bulls to progressive cattle rearers and trained cow servants. Through this scheme, Murrah bulls are provided with a 75% subsidy to animal keepers of all categories. The applications for this scheme are processed through a multi-level approval system starting from Gram Sabha. The scheme is applicable in all districts of the state.
eligibility: The applicant should be a progressive cattle breeder or a trained cow-helper.
The applicant should be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant should be willing to maintain and breed the Murrah bull.

offline: by the beneficiaries in Gram Sabha.
by the Janpad Panchayat for Gram Sabha-approved beneficiaries.
by the Agriculture Standing Committee of the District Panchayat.
The interested applicants should contact the nearest Veterinary Officer of the concerned District Incharge of Animal Dispensary / Deputy Director of Veterinary.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the Advanced Animal Breeding Scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to supply breedable pedigree Murrah bulls to progressive cattle breeders or trained cow-helpers to improve livestock quality and milk production.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is available to all sections of society, provided the applicant is a progressive cattle breeder or a trained cow-helper.

faqs.question: What is the unit cost for the Murrah bull?
faqs.answer: The unit cost for the breedable pedigree Murrah bull is Rs. 60,000, which includes transportation and Rs. 2,000 for insurance.

faqs.question: What percentage of the cost is covered by the grant?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides a 75% grant, while the beneficiary is required to pay the remaining 25% of the cost.

faqs.question: Can anyone from any section of society apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme is open to all sections of society, and there are no caste or category restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the benefit provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries receive a breedable pedigree Murrah bull at a subsidized rate, helping improve cattle breeding practices and milk production.

faqs.question: Is there any financial assistance for transportation and insurance?
faqs.answer: Yes, transportation and insurance costs are included in the unit cost of Rs. 60,000.

faqs.question: Where is the scheme applicable?
faqs.answer: The scheme is applicable in all districts of Madhya Pradesh.

faqs.question: Can I use the Murrah bull for commercial purposes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Murrah bull is intended to improve breeding and milk production, and it can be used for commercial purposes in line with the scheme’s objectives.
